rationale: The citing paper directly engages Goldsmith and Amir (2010) as conflicting evidence, stating that its own finding (risk-associated frames reduce WTP) is at odds with their result that uncertain promotions can be as attractive as the best certain outcome. It discusses this discrepancy in detail, including possible reasons (different dependent measure and add-on framing) rather than listing the citation as background. The paper also links Goldsmith and Amir to its broader interpretation of when the uncertainty effect does or does not appear.
relation_description: The citing paper challenges the implication from Goldsmith and Amir (2010) that uncertainty-based promotions can retain valued-outcome attractiveness, by showing robust WTP reductions under risk-associated frames. It treats the prior finding as a contrast case and argues the discrepancy is not fully explained by add-on framing in its own follow-up test.
